summary: Tested tax and refund functionality for partner orders/subscriptions and customer manual orders. All 9 features from the review request were verified. All 11 backend tests PASS. All UI flows functional with 2 design/functional issues found.

ui_bugs.issue: 'partially_refunded' status is missing from STATUS_COLORS in PartnerOrdersTab.tsx. Badge renders with fallback bg-slate-100 (white/light-gray, no distinct color), making it hard to distinguish from an empty/blank badge visually.
ui_bugs.fix: Add `partially_refunded: 'bg-orange-100 text-orange-700'` to STATUS_COLORS in PartnerOrdersTab.tsx

ui_bugs.issue: 'partially_refunded' is NOT in the STATUSES array (`['pending', 'unpaid', 'paid', 'cancelled', 'refunded']`). When editing a partially_refunded order, the status combobox shows blank, unable to display or preserve the current status. The user could accidentally overwrite it.
ui_bugs.fix: Add 'partially_refunded' to STATUSES array and add display label for it in PartnerOrdersTab.tsx

issues: HTML hydration console error: <span> as child of <tbody> and <tr> as child of <span> - caused by conditional rendering inside table bodies. This is a React rendering anti-pattern that can cause hydration mismatches.

In the customer orders refund dialog, 'Already Refunded' is shown as `(selectedOrder.refunded_amount || 0) / 100`, dividing by 100 - implying customer orders store refunded_amount in cents. Verify this is intentional and consistent with how refunded_amount is stored in customer orders backend.

action_items: HIGH: Add 'partially_refunded' to STATUSES array in PartnerOrdersTab.tsx so that edit mode can display and preserve the status
MEDIUM: Add 'partially_refunded: bg-orange-100 text-orange-700' to STATUS_COLORS in PartnerOrdersTab.tsx for proper badge coloring
LOW: Fix HTML table structure warning - conditional rendering wrapping <tr>/<tbody> with <span> causes hydration errors. Use Fragment or fix conditional rendering pattern.
VERIFY: Confirm whether customer orders store refunded_amount in cents (requires /100) or dollars - the division by 100 in OrdersTab.tsx refund dialog may be a bug if stored in dollars
critical_code_review_comments: PartnerOrdersTab.tsx: STATUSES = ['pending', 'unpaid', 'paid', 'cancelled', 'refunded'] is missing 'partially_refunded'. This means the edit dialog status dropdown is blank for partially_refunded orders - a functional bug that could lead to accidental status overwrites.
PartnerOrdersTab.tsx: STATUS_COLORS missing 'partially_refunded' key - renders as unstyled badge.
PartnerOrdersTab.tsx/PartnerSubscriptionsTab.tsx: <span> rendered as child of <tbody> from conditional loading/empty state rendering - causes React hydration console errors.

success_rate.frontend: 90% (2 design/functional issues found, all core flows work)

test_credentials: admin@automateaccounts.local / ChangeMe123! (partner code: automate-accounts, platform admin)
seed_data_creation: Created test partner orders: TEST Tax Order UI (paid, HST 13%), TEST_Tax Order, TEST_Paid Order For Refund (fully refunded), TEST_RefundExceed Order, TEST_Unpaid Order No Refund. Created TEST_Tax Subscription via backend tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tax fields (tax_name, tax_rate, tax_amount) work correctly for partner orders, partner subscriptions, and customer manual orders. Refund flow works end-to-end: paid order gets RotateCcw button, refund dialog shows order total/already refunded/available to refund/amount input/reason select/provider select, partial refund → partially_refunded status, full refund → refunded status. API validation (400 for overpayment/unpaid orders) works. Two issues: (1) 'partially_refunded' missing from STATUSES array in PartnerOrdersTab.tsx causing blank status in edit mode, (2) 'partially_refunded' missing from STATUS_COLORS causing unstyled badge.

partially_refunded_missing_from_statuses.description: When a refund is processed via the refund dialog, the backend correctly sets status to 'partially_refunded'. However, the frontend PartnerOrdersTab.tsx STATUSES constant does not include 'partially_refunded'. When opening the edit dialog for such an order, the Select component cannot match the current value to any option, resulting in a blank display.
partially_refunded_missing_from_statuses.reproduction: 1. Create a paid partner order. 2. Click the refund button and process a partial refund. 3. Click the edit (pencil) button on the now-partially_refunded order. 4. Observe the Status dropdown is blank.
partially_refunded_missing_from_statuses.mitigation: Add 'partially_refunded' to the STATUSES array in PartnerOrdersTab.tsx and add a corresponding entry to STATUS_COLORS.
